title: A button that is not a button
testCase: Is there a real submit button, and the right type on the other one?
blocks.0.text: The form has two buttons. One says "Order now", the other "Reset". Run it and see which one works.
blocks.1.text: Neither does what you expect. Both are wrong, each in its own way.
blocks.2.text: The first: a <div> pretending to be a button
blocks.3.text: "Order now" is a <div>. It does not press, it does nothing. Add CSS to make it look like a button and JavaScript to catch the click — and it will work. People do exactly that every day.
blocks.4.text: And it is still broken. Here is what you lose:
blocks.5.items.0: You cannot Tab to it. Someone who does not use a mouse will never reach it.
blocks.5.items.1: It does not respond to Enter or Space.
blocks.5.items.2: A screen reader says "group", not "button". The person does not know there is anything to press.
blocks.5.items.3: It does not submit the form. You will need JavaScript to do something <button> does on its own.
blocks.6.text: You write twenty lines to rebuild something you were given for free.
blocks.7.text: The second: a button with the wrong job
blocks.8.text: "Reset" is a real <button> and that is exactly why it is dangerous. Try it: fill in the email and press it.
blocks.9.text: It submitted the form. And you called it "Reset". Because a <button> INSIDE a form submits by default — that is its job, unless you tell it otherwise.
blocks.11.items.0: type="submit" — submits the form. This is the default.
blocks.11.items.1: type="reset" — clears every field. Rarely used and people hate it — they hit it by mistake and lose their work.
blocks.11.items.2: type="button" — does NOTHING on its own. For buttons you will drive with JavaScript.
blocks.13.text: Always write the type, even when it is submit. Otherwise the next person reads your code and cannot tell whether you meant it or forgot.
blocks.16.text: Something you press that DOES something → <button>. Something you press that GOES somewhere → <a>. Never a <div>.
blocks.17.text: If you ever catch yourself writing <div onclick=...> — stop. There is a tag for that and it works better than anything you will write.
blocks.18.text: Your turn. Make "Order now" a real button that submits. And fix the other one — it should not be submitting anything.
